title: `links config / open / status` — utility commands
description: Implement three utility commands that round out the CLI experience: config (edit links.yaml in $EDITOR), open (open the deployed page in browser), and status (show current config summary).

features: Find links.yaml via findConfig(), open it in $EDITOR (fallback to $VISUAL, then 'nano', then 'vi')
Uses child_process.spawnSync to open editor in the current terminal (inherit stdio)
If links.yaml not found: print 'No links.yaml found. Run: links init' and exit 1
Default: open config.domain in browser using the 'open' npm package
--local: open dist/index.html (or --out path from last deploy) as a file:// URL instead
If no domain set and no --local: print 'No domain set. Use --local to preview, or set domain in links.yaml' and exit 1
Read links.yaml, display a summary: name, bio (truncated), theme, domain (or 'not set'), total links, scheduled links count, avatar (set/not set)
Also show: last modified timestamp of links.yaml
Output: clean formatted text, no table library needed, chalk for label coloring
